data.body: CI (Verified -1) on patchset 1 surfaced emergent work: enabling verify.overlap_enabled=true broke 7 tests in tests/unit/test_enrich_drain.py. Root cause: the write-path drain calls maybe_drain(tracker) with repo_root=None, so its overlap gate reads the AMBIENT checkout config via cwd — which this change now sets to overlap_enabled=true. So every create_ticket/enqueue during those tests' setup spawned a drain child, polluting the _spawn_detached_drain spies (['spawn'] != []) and racing/emptying the queue in test_batch_cap (processed 0 != 5). These tests implicitly relied on the default overlap_enabled=false. Fix (lands in the SAME commit — the config flip breaks CI without it): pin REBAR_LLM_OVERLAP_DRAIN=off in the module's repo fixture so setup writes never auto-drain; tests that exercise a drain mode still opt in via _mock_flags(drain=...)/direct D.drain. Both test_enrich_drain.py (20) and test_plan_review_overlap.py (13) pass locally after the fix.
